visa_types.name: Business
visa_types.documents_required:
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation for the omission.
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
Business Visa Checklist 
Return Courier Form
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Business]
The visa fee is an administration fee which covers the cost of processing your application. This fee cannot be refunded if your application is refused or withdrawn.

[Business2]
Payment:
Applicants are required to deposit the required Visa Fee, VFS Global service fee and Return Courier Fee into our bank account. All three fees can be paid at once. Please note that, a signed and stamped original receipt must be submitted along with the visa documents.
Details for bank transfer:
Bank Account Name: Gateway Management Lojistik A.Ş.
Bank name: GARANTI BBVA
BIC/SWIFT Code: TGBATRIS
IBAN Number: TR06 0006 2001 6710 0009 0894 03 (EUR)
You must include your name, surname and GW1000 note in the explanation part of the payment.
Please send your visa application file to below address:
VFS GLOBAL
NON SCHENGEN – IRELAND DEPARTMENT
ISCI BLOKLARI MAHALLESI, MUHSIN YAZICIOGLU CADDESI, NO:57/A ,
REGNUM SKY TOWER, 06530, CANKAYA – ANKARA, TURKIYE
Visa Fee Exemptions

Some applicants are exempt from the requirement to pay the visa fee. Check below if the visa fee does not apply to you.
Nationalities exempt from visa fees

[Business3]
Family members of Irish Citizens who are exempt from visa fees (on production of the required documents) 
- Spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse with Irish Nationality)
- Separated spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse and their Irish Nationality) - subject to judicial separation
- Widow/widower (subject to death certificate)
- Child (under 18 years)
- Adopted child (under 18 years - subject to adoption papers)
Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees
Spouse registered as a student or in employment (subject to submission of marriage certificate, passport of EEA citizen and, if applicable, details of educational institution)
Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse
- Child ( under 21 years)
- Child (under 21 years) of the spouse
- Adopted child (subject to adoption papers)
- Dependent parent
- Dependent parent of the spouse
- Other dependent family members in the direct ascending line ( e.g. grandparent) or descending line (e.g. grandchild)
- Other dependent family members of the spouse in the direct ascending (e.g. grandparent) or descending line ( e.g. grandchild)
Other persons who are exempt from visa fees
- Holders of diplomatic passports
- Programme Refugees (within the meaning of the Refugee Act 1996)
All references to 'spouse' also apply to a civil partner i.e. a person who has contracted a registered partnership, or is a party to a class of legal relationship specified in an order under section 5 of the Civil Partnership and Certain Rights and Obligations of Cohabitants Act 2010 as entitled to be recognized as a civil partnership.
Please Note:
- There is a service charge of 50 Euro
- There is a mandatory return courrier (Turkey to Turkmenistan) fee of 100 Euro
- The Visa Fee has to be paid online.
Note:
All fees (Visa fee, Service fee, Return Courrier fee) are non-refundable and may only be paid online.
visa_types.processing_time: Applicants may apply up to three months before their intended travel dates. You may reach the most recent processing times from the Irish Department of Foreign Affair’s website : https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/ (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
You can apply for this visa if you are working outside Ireland and want to come to Ireland to carry out activities relating to your job, such as to attend meetings, attend trade shows for promotional work, arrange deals or negotiate or sign trade agreements or contracts, or carry out fact finding missions. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
You can apply for this visa if you are working outside Ireland and want to come to Ireland to carry out activities relating to your job, such as to attend meetings, attend trade shows for promotional work, arrange deals or negotiate or sign trade agreements or contracts, or carry out fact finding missions. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

## WHO SHOULD APPLY?
All Turkmenistan passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
If you are not a Turkmenistan passport holder, please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Pages/Countries%20whose%20nationals%20are%20visa%20required)
Please amend the following paragraph to read:
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Visa Waiver Programme, may be able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K.
Regarding visa applications for all Libyan Nationals please click here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/our-services/visas/visas-for-ireland/).

## Visa Fees

[Business]
The visa fee is an administration fee which covers the cost of processing your application. This fee cannot be refunded if your application is refused or withdrawn.

[Business2]
Payment:
Applicants are required to deposit the required Visa Fee, VFS Global service fee and Return Courier Fee into our bank account. All three fees can be paid at once. Please note that, a signed and stamped original receipt must be submitted along with the visa documents.
Details for bank transfer:
Bank Account Name: Gateway Management Lojistik A.Ş.
Bank name: GARANTI BBVA
BIC/SWIFT Code: TGBATRIS
IBAN Number: TR06 0006 2001 6710 0009 0894 03 (EUR)
You must include your name, surname and GW1000 note in the explanation part of the payment.
Please send your visa application file to below address:
VFS GLOBAL
NON SCHENGEN – IRELAND DEPARTMENT
ISCI BLOKLARI MAHALLESI, MUHSIN YAZICIOGLU CADDESI, NO:57/A ,
REGNUM SKY TOWER, 06530, CANKAYA – ANKARA, TURKIYE
Visa Fee Exemptions

Some applicants are exempt from the requirement to pay the visa fee. Check below if the visa fee does not apply to you.
Nationalities exempt from visa fees

[Business3]
Family members of Irish Citizens who are exempt from visa fees (on production of the required documents) 
- Spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse with Irish Nationality)
- Separated spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse and their Irish Nationality) - subject to judicial separation
- Widow/widower (subject to death certificate)
- Child (under 18 years)
- Adopted child (under 18 years - subject to adoption papers)
Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees
Spouse registered as a student or in employment (subject to submission of marriage certificate, passport of EEA citizen and, if applicable, details of educational institution)
Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse
- Child ( under 21 years)
- Child (under 21 years) of the spouse
- Adopted child (subject to adoption papers)
- Dependent parent
- Dependent parent of the spouse
- Other dependent family members in the direct ascending line ( e.g. grandparent) or descending line (e.g. grandchild)
- Other dependent family members of the spouse in the direct ascending (e.g. grandparent) or descending line ( e.g. grandchild)
Other persons who are exempt from visa fees
- Holders of diplomatic passports
- Programme Refugees (within the meaning of the Refugee Act 1996)
All references to 'spouse' also apply to a civil partner i.e. a person who has contracted a registered partnership, or is a party to a class of legal relationship specified in an order under section 5 of the Civil Partnership and Certain Rights and Obligations of Cohabitants Act 2010 as entitled to be recognized as a civil partnership.
Please Note:
- There is a service charge of 50 Euro
- There is a mandatory return courrier (Turkey to Turkmenistan) fee of 100 Euro
- The Visa Fee has to be paid online.
Note:
All fees (Visa fee, Service fee, Return Courrier fee) are non-refundable and may only be paid online.

## Documents Required
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation for the omission.
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
Business Visa Checklist 
Return Courier Form

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Applicants may apply up to three months before their intended travel dates. You may reach the most recent processing times from the Irish Department of Foreign Affair’s website : https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/ (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/)

## Online form and questionnaire

The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click on the link: www.visas.inis.gov.ie (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx)
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office as indicated by the on-line system.
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form.

Information on completing online application is available to download here in English.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to come to Ireland to reside for longer than 3 months with a family member who is an Irish citizen or who is lawfully resident in Ireland you can apply for a long stay (join family) visa.
A comprehensive statement of national immigration policy on the area of family reunification is contained in the Policy Document on Non-EEA Family Reunification (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Family%20Reunification%20Policy%20Document.pdf/Files/Family%20Reunification%20Policy%20Document.pdf) published on 31 December 2013.
The policies outlined in the document apply to all decision making in relation to family reunification cases where ministerial discretion applies including long stay (join family) visa applications.
The policies do not apply to:
- applications pursuant to Directive 2504/38/EC on the rights of citizens of the Union and their family members to move and reside freely within the territory of Member States
- applications for family reunification by recognised refugees or persons granted subsidiary protection.
Before you apply for a long stay (join family) visa please read the Policy Document carefully to find out:
- who is eligible to be a sponsor for the purposes of an application for family reunification,
- who may be sponsored and the eligibility requirements,
- whether waiting periods apply before a person becomes eligible to sponsor,
- whether a sponsor must achieve a minimum level of earnings prior to being eligible to sponsor,
- what a person who is granted a join family visa may do in Ireland,
- how dependency is measured when this is adduced in support of an application for family reunification.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to come to Ireland to reside for longer than 3 months with a family member who is an Irish citizen or who is lawfully resident in Ireland you can apply for a long stay (join family) visa.
A comprehensive statement of national immigration policy on the area of family reunification is contained in the Policy Document on Non-EEA Family Reunification (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Family%20Reunification%20Policy%20Document.pdf/Files/Family%20Reunification%20Policy%20Document.pdf) published on 31 December 2013.
The policies outlined in the document apply to all decision making in relation to family reunification cases where ministerial discretion applies including long stay (join family) visa applications.
The policies do not apply to:
- applications pursuant to Directive 2504/38/EC on the rights of citizens of the Union and their family members to move and reside freely within the territory of Member States
- applications for family reunification by recognised refugees or persons granted subsidiary protection.
Before you apply for a long stay (join family) visa please read the Policy Document carefully to find out:
- who is eligible to be a sponsor for the purposes of an application for family reunification,
- who may be sponsored and the eligibility requirements,
- whether waiting periods apply before a person becomes eligible to sponsor,
- whether a sponsor must achieve a minimum level of earnings prior to being eligible to sponsor,
- what a person who is granted a join family visa may do in Ireland,
- how dependency is measured when this is adduced in support of an application for family reunification.

## WHO SHOULD APPLY?
All Turkmenistan passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
If you are not a Turkmenistan passport holder, please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Pages/Countries%20whose%20nationals%20are%20visa%20required)
Please amend the following paragraph to read:
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Visa Waiver Programme, may be able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K.
Regarding visa applications for all Libyan Nationals please click here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/our-services/visas/visas-for-ireland/).

## Visa Fees

[Business]
The visa fee is an administration fee which covers the cost of processing your application. This fee cannot be refunded if your application is refused or withdrawn.

[Business2]
Payment:
Applicants are required to deposit the required Visa Fee, VFS Global service fee and Return Courier Fee into our bank account. All three fees can be paid at once. Please note that, a signed and stamped original receipt must be submitted along with the visa documents.
Details for bank transfer:
Bank Account Name: Gateway Management Lojistik A.Ş.
Bank name: GARANTI BBVA
BIC/SWIFT Code: TGBATRIS
IBAN Number: TR06 0006 2001 6710 0009 0894 03 (EUR)
You must include your name, surname and GW1000 note in the explanation part of the payment.
Please send your visa application file to below address:
VFS GLOBAL
NON SCHENGEN – IRELAND DEPARTMENT
ISCI BLOKLARI MAHALLESI, MUHSIN YAZICIOGLU CADDESI, NO:57/A ,
REGNUM SKY TOWER, 06530, CANKAYA – ANKARA, TURKIYE
Visa Fee Exemptions

Some applicants are exempt from the requirement to pay the visa fee. Check below if the visa fee does not apply to you.
Nationalities exempt from visa fees

[Business3]
Family members of Irish Citizens who are exempt from visa fees (on production of the required documents) 
- Spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse with Irish Nationality)
- Separated spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse and their Irish Nationality) - subject to judicial separation
- Widow/widower (subject to death certificate)
- Child (under 18 years)
- Adopted child (under 18 years - subject to adoption papers)
Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees
Spouse registered as a student or in employment (subject to submission of marriage certificate, passport of EEA citizen and, if applicable, details of educational institution)
Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse
- Child ( under 21 years)
- Child (under 21 years) of the spouse
- Adopted child (subject to adoption papers)
- Dependent parent
- Dependent parent of the spouse
- Other dependent family members in the direct ascending line ( e.g. grandparent) or descending line (e.g. grandchild)
- Other dependent family members of the spouse in the direct ascending (e.g. grandparent) or descending line ( e.g. grandchild)
Other persons who are exempt from visa fees
- Holders of diplomatic passports
- Programme Refugees (within the meaning of the Refugee Act 1996)
All references to 'spouse' also apply to a civil partner i.e. a person who has contracted a registered partnership, or is a party to a class of legal relationship specified in an order under section 5 of the Civil Partnership and Certain Rights and Obligations of Cohabitants Act 2010 as entitled to be recognized as a civil partnership.
Please Note:
- There is a service charge of 50 Euro
- There is a mandatory return courrier (Turkey to Turkmenistan) fee of 100 Euro
- The Visa Fee has to be paid online.
Note:
All fees (Visa fee, Service fee, Return Courrier fee) are non-refundable and may only be paid online.

## Documents Required
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation for the omission.
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
Important Note: Please note that Join Family applications are processed at the headquarters of the Irish Naturalisation and Immigration Service in Dublin. As this office does not have Turkish speaking staff, all documents should be notarised translation into English. Two copies of all documents should be provided.
Join family Irish Citizen Spouse-Parent Visa Checklist
Join spouse EU Citizen Visa Checklist 
Marriage/Civil Partnership Visa 
Return Courier Form

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Applicants may apply up to three months before their intended travel dates. You may reach the most recent processing times from the Irish Department of Foreign Affair’s website : https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/ (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/)

## Online form and questionnaire

The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click on the link: www.visas.inis.gov.ie (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx)
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office as indicated by the on-line system.
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form.

Information on completing online application is available to download here in English.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
You can apply for a study visa if you are planning to come to Ireland to undertake a course of study.
You are advised to familiarise yourself with our Policy on Non-EEA Nationals (insert hyperlink: Click here (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Pages/Student%20Guidelines%20-%20New%20Immigration%20Regime%20for%20Full%20Time%20non-EEA%20Students)) studying in Ireland and ensure that you fulfil the requirement of our policy before you make your visa application.
You can apply for a study visa up to 3 months before your date of travel to Ireland.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
You can apply for a study visa if you are planning to come to Ireland to undertake a course of study.
You are advised to familiarise yourself with our Policy on Non-EEA Nationals (insert hyperlink: Click here (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Pages/Student%20Guidelines%20-%20New%20Immigration%20Regime%20for%20Full%20Time%20non-EEA%20Students)) studying in Ireland and ensure that you fulfil the requirement of our policy before you make your visa application.
You can apply for a study visa up to 3 months before your date of travel to Ireland.

## WHO SHOULD APPLY?
All Turkmenistan passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
If you are not a Turkmenistan passport holder, please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Pages/Countries%20whose%20nationals%20are%20visa%20required)
Please amend the following paragraph to read:
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Visa Waiver Programme, may be able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K.
Regarding visa applications for all Libyan Nationals please click here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/our-services/visas/visas-for-ireland/).

## Visa Fees

[Business]
The visa fee is an administration fee which covers the cost of processing your application. This fee cannot be refunded if your application is refused or withdrawn.

[Business2]
Payment:
Applicants are required to deposit the required Visa Fee, VFS Global service fee and Return Courier Fee into our bank account. All three fees can be paid at once. Please note that, a signed and stamped original receipt must be submitted along with the visa documents.
Details for bank transfer:
Bank Account Name: Gateway Management Lojistik A.Ş.
Bank name: GARANTI BBVA
BIC/SWIFT Code: TGBATRIS
IBAN Number: TR06 0006 2001 6710 0009 0894 03 (EUR)
You must include your name, surname and GW1000 note in the explanation part of the payment.
Please send your visa application file to below address:
VFS GLOBAL
NON SCHENGEN – IRELAND DEPARTMENT
ISCI BLOKLARI MAHALLESI, MUHSIN YAZICIOGLU CADDESI, NO:57/A ,
REGNUM SKY TOWER, 06530, CANKAYA – ANKARA, TURKIYE
Visa Fee Exemptions

Some applicants are exempt from the requirement to pay the visa fee. Check below if the visa fee does not apply to you.
Nationalities exempt from visa fees

[Business3]
Family members of Irish Citizens who are exempt from visa fees (on production of the required documents) 
- Spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse with Irish Nationality)
- Separated spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse and their Irish Nationality) - subject to judicial separation
- Widow/widower (subject to death certificate)
- Child (under 18 years)
- Adopted child (under 18 years - subject to adoption papers)
Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees
Spouse registered as a student or in employment (subject to submission of marriage certificate, passport of EEA citizen and, if applicable, details of educational institution)
Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse
- Child ( under 21 years)
- Child (under 21 years) of the spouse
- Adopted child (subject to adoption papers)
- Dependent parent
- Dependent parent of the spouse
- Other dependent family members in the direct ascending line ( e.g. grandparent) or descending line (e.g. grandchild)
- Other dependent family members of the spouse in the direct ascending (e.g. grandparent) or descending line ( e.g. grandchild)
Other persons who are exempt from visa fees
- Holders of diplomatic passports
- Programme Refugees (within the meaning of the Refugee Act 1996)
All references to 'spouse' also apply to a civil partner i.e. a person who has contracted a registered partnership, or is a party to a class of legal relationship specified in an order under section 5 of the Civil Partnership and Certain Rights and Obligations of Cohabitants Act 2010 as entitled to be recognized as a civil partnership.
Please Note:
- There is a service charge of 50 Euro
- There is a mandatory return courrier (Turkey to Turkmenistan) fee of 100 Euro
- The Visa Fee has to be paid online.
Note:
All fees (Visa fee, Service fee, Return Courrier fee) are non-refundable and may only be paid online.

## Documents Required
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation for the omission.
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
Student Visa Checklist (Short term) 
Student Visa Checklist Long Term
Return Courier Form

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Applicants may apply up to three months before their intended travel dates. You may reach the most recent processing times from the Irish Department of Foreign Affair’s website : https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/ (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/)

## Download Forms
Please kindly donwload Questionnaire form on PDF format, fill it and include between your visa application documents.
All applicants for long stay Irish study visas must complete Financial Summary Form
The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click on the link: www.visas.inis.gov.ie (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx)
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office as indicated by the on-line system.
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form.

Information on completing online application is available to download here in English.

Details for bank transfer:
Bank Account Name: Gateway Management Lojistik A.Ş.
Bank name: GARANTI BBVA
BIC/SWIFT Code: TGBATRIS
IBAN Number: TR06 0006 2001 6710 0009 0894 03 (EUR)
You must include your name, surname and GW1000 note in the explanation part of the payment.
Please send your visa application file to below address:
VFS GLOBAL
NON SCHENGEN – IRELAND DEPARTMENT
ISCI BLOKLARI MAHALLESI, MUHSIN YAZICIOGLU CADDESI, NO:57/A ,
REGNUM SKY TOWER, 06530, CANKAYA – ANKARA, TURKIYE
Visa Fee Exemptions

Some applicants are exempt from the requirement to pay the visa fee. Check below if the visa fee does not apply to you.
Nationalities exempt from visa fees

[Business3]
Family members of Irish Citizens who are exempt from visa fees (on production of the required documents) 
- Spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse with Irish Nationality)
- Separated spouse (where the permission to reside is based on the Spouse and their Irish Nationality) - subject to judicial separation
- Widow/widower (subject to death certificate)
- Child (under 18 years)
- Adopted child (under 18 years - subject to adoption papers)
Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees
Spouse registered as a student or in employment (subject to submission of marriage certificate, passport of EEA citizen and, if applicable, details of educational institution)
Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse
- Child ( under 21 years)
- Child (under 21 years) of the spouse
- Adopted child (subject to adoption papers)
- Dependent parent
- Dependent parent of the spouse
- Other dependent family members in the direct ascending line ( e.g. grandparent) or descending line (e.g. grandchild)
- Other dependent family members of the spouse in the direct ascending (e.g. grandparent) or descending line ( e.g. grandchild)
Other persons who are exempt from visa fees
- Holders of diplomatic passports
- Programme Refugees (within the meaning of the Refugee Act 1996)
All references to 'spouse' also apply to a civil partner i.e. a person who has contracted a registered partnership, or is a party to a class of legal relationship specified in an order under section 5 of the Civil Partnership and Certain Rights and Obligations of Cohabitants Act 2010 as entitled to be recognized as a civil partnership.
Please Note:
- There is a service charge of 50 Euro
- There is a mandatory return courrier (Turkey to Turkmenistan) fee of 100 Euro
- The Visa Fee has to be paid online.
Note:
All fees (Visa fee, Service fee, Return Courrier fee) are non-refundable and may only be paid online.

visa_types.forms:
The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click on the link: www.visas.inis.gov.ie (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx)
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office as indicated by the on-line system.
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form.

Information on completing online application is available to download here in English.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
You can apply for this visa if you want to travel to Ireland for a conference, relating to your work, studies, or for an event. On this page you will find useful guidelines and details of requirements for Conference Visa application.
An applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
You can apply for this visa if you want to travel to Ireland for a conference, relating to your work, studies, or for an event. On this page you will find useful guidelines and details of requirements for Conference Visa application.
An applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

## WHO SHOULD APPLY?
All Turkmenistan passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
If you are not a Turkmenistan passport holder, please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (http://www.inis.gov.ie/en/INIS/Pages/Countries%20whose%20nationals%20are%20visa%20required)
Please amend the following paragraph to read:
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Visa Waiver Programme, may be able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K.
Regarding visa applications for all Libyan Nationals please click here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/our-services/visas/visas-for-ireland/).

## Visa Fees

[Business]
The visa fee is an administration fee which covers the cost of processing your application. This fee cannot be refunded if your application is refused or withdrawn.

[Business2]
Payment:
Applicants are required to deposit the required Visa Fee, VFS Global service fee and Return Courier Fee into our bank account. All three fees can be paid at once. Please note that, a signed and stamped original receipt must be submitted along with the visa documents.

Family member of EEA citizen who is exempt from visa fees

Beneficiaries of Directive 2004/38/EC (Free Movement Directive) i.e. ‘qualifying family members’ of EU/EEA /Swiss Citizens are exempt from visa fees.
- Spouse

All references to 'spouse' also apply to a civil partner i.e. a person who has contracted a registered partnership, or is a party to a class of legal relationship specified in an order under section 5 of the Civil Partnership and Certain Rights and Obligations of Cohabitants Act 2010 as entitled to be recognized as a civil partnership.

## Documents Required
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation for the omission.
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
Conference checklist 
Return Courier Form

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Applicants may apply up to three months before their intended travel dates. You may reach the most recent processing times from the Irish Department of Foreign Affair’s website : https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/ (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/turkey/visas/visas-for-ireland-weekly-decisions-report/)

## Download Forms
The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click on the link: www.visas.inis.gov.ie (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx)
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office as indicated by the on-line system.
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form.

Information on completing online application is available to download here in English.
